10. Judas. Luke calls him "Judas the brother of James," and Matthew names him as "Lebbeus, whose surname was Thaddeus." As to his works and ministry we are not advised, and conclude only that he was of like spiritual stature with his brethren of the Twelve.

Although referred to as Thomas, the main part of the apostle's name is Judas, or Jude. Thomas is a Syriac or Aramaic word that means "twin," and Didymus a Greek word that also means "twin," producing the name Didymus Thomas (see John 11:16). His name, then, means "Jude the twin."
